title: payment — field classification
description: Payment carries customer identifiers (email, phone, billing address), amounts (revenue-sensitive), payment method tokens (RESTRICTED — provider credentials + card data equivalents), risk scores + fraud signals. Zero card PAN/CVV/magnetic-stripe/PIN — those are PCI-DSS scope and NEVER touched (customer confirms via provider SDK client-side). Billing details snapshot is PII + retained per financial retention (7y / 10y). Dispute evidence is confidential — competitor + regulator sensitivity.

confidential: Payment.amount_cents (revenue — competitor tenant cross-inference risk; bucketed on egress in analytics)
Payment.device_fingerprint_hash (already SHA-256; still competitor + fraud-network signal)
Payment.billing_details_snapshot (PII: name + email + phone + billing address — frozen at capture)
PaymentMethod.billing_address (PII: address at method attach time)
PaymentMethod.fingerprint_hash (SHA-256 of card — enables duplicate detection; competitor sensitivity via correlation attacks against known hashes)
PaymentIntent.last_error_message (may leak account structure — Stripe error messages sometimes reference account IDs)
restricted: PaymentMethod.provider_reference_id (ENCRYPTED AT REST — provider token: pm_..., payment_method_id, storedPaymentMethodId, CARD_NONCE-*, payment_method, caller-defined. Effectively a bearer credential — leaking it enables an attacker to charge the customer's card on our behalf. NEVER decrypted in logs, notifications, broadcasts, or wire projections. Only the driver factory reads decrypted values.)
PaymentIntent.provider_reference_id (ENCRYPTED AT REST — provider intent: pi_..., txn_..., session-id, payment-id, order_...)

PaymentIntent.client_secret (ENCRYPTED AT REST — client-side confirmation secret. Distinct encryption key from provider_reference_id. Passed to the customer's SDK — MUST be TLS-terminated on egress to prevent leakage.)
Payment.provider_metadata (ENCRYPTED AT REST — full provider response; may contain provider-specific account identifiers)
regulated_minor: PaymentMethod.customer_id when customer_type = 'sports::Athlete' — athlete may be a minor; the payment method is parent-managed (parent's UI attaches on athlete profile via delegation). Consent-tier + retention still per parent's regime but ADDITIONAL restrictions on marketing signals (never fire PaymentCaptured attribution when the underlying athlete is a minor without explicit parental consent — the growth::marketing module handles this per consent snapshot)
regulated_financial: Payment.amount_cents + currency + fee_cents + net_cents (financial audit — 7-year retention; 10y Enterprise)
PaymentDispute.amount_cents + evidence (regulator + provider dispute record retention: 7y baseline; 10y Enterprise)
audit_log: PaymentDispute.outcome (terminal state audit — feeds compliance reporting)

notes: PaymentMethod.provider_reference_id + PaymentIntent.provider_reference_id + PaymentIntent.client_secret + Payment.provider_metadata + PaymentDispute.provider_reference_id + PaymentDispute.evidence are ALL encrypted at rest via TokenEncryptor (AES-256-GCM). Encryption keys managed by the compliance/encryption service. Distinct keys per field family for defense-in-depth.

The client_secret specifically must ALSO be transmitted over TLS on egress — it's a bearer credential the customer's SDK uses to confirm client-side. Leaking it enables an attacker to complete the confirmation flow on someone else's intent.
PCI-DSS scope: NO card / PAN / CVV / magnetic-stripe / PIN fields on ANY payment row. Payment.payment_method_last4 is the LAST 4 digits ONLY (PCI-DSS scope-out).

The billing_details_snapshot is PII (name / email / phone / address) retained for the payment's lifetime (7y / 10y). GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) contractual basis — customers cannot opt out of a payment record they're party to.

Payment.amount_cents is CONFIDENTIAL, not restricted — the tier bucket exposes the fact of revenue existing (e.g. tenant admin sees exact amounts of their own tenant; support role sees ranges; platform_ops sees exact with audit trail). Analytics NEVER carry raw amounts — always bucketed.

regulated_financial tagging applies to every revenue-carrying field. The retention pipeline honours the 7-year rule (10y Enterprise) on this class. UserErased redacts subject-identifying fields but preserves the financial rows per Art. 17 §3.

regulated_minor: athlete-linked payment methods carry an additional guardrail — the growth::marketing module checks consent_snapshot for parental-consent before dispatching marketing signals. Direct payment records are NOT restricted by minor status (Art. 6(1)(b) contract with the parent).

PaymentDispute.evidence is CONFIDENTIAL — it may contain customer signature files + internal communication logs. Support role sees a redacted view (evidence fields nulled). Admin + finance role sees the full evidence. Platform_ops requires explicit compliance approval workflow (platform.payment.pii.view permission) to see un-redacted evidence.
